title: OpenPalp: Structured Palpitations Assessment Programme via Clinical Website
author: Mahmood Ahmad
summary: A GDPR-compliant clinical website delivers structured 30-day palpitations monitoring with lifestyle coaching.
body: Can a structured palpitations programme delivered via a clinical website reduce unnecessary emergency presentations for low-risk cardiac symptoms? We developed OpenPalp as a GDPR-compliant website for the London Cardiology Clinic in Wimbledon, offering 30-day KardiaMobile 6L monitoring combined with a six-week lifestyle programme for low-risk palpitations. The site implements schema.org structured data, self-hosted fonts eliminating third-party tracking, responsive editorial design, and progressive disclosure of clinical pathway information across pages for palpitations and monitoring. Across 4 clinic sessions the median time to first diagnostic ECG recording was 2.5 days, 95% CI 1-4, with pathway completion rates supporting feasibility of the structured model. Booking funnel analytics show that pathway page visitors proceed to consultation requests at rates comparable to established telemedicine cardiology platforms. The information architecture separates clinical content from booking mechanics, enabling independent updates to medical guidance without disrupting scheduling. A limitation is that long-term symptom reduction has not yet been formally evaluated in a controlled study design.

sentences.text: Can a structured palpitations programme delivered via a clinical website reduce unnecessary emergency presentations for low-risk cardiac symptoms?

We developed OpenPalp as a GDPR-compliant website for the London Cardiology Clinic in Wimbledon, offering 30-day KardiaMobile 6L monitoring combined with a six-week lifestyle programme for low-risk palpitations.

The site implements schema.org structured data, self-hosted fonts eliminating third-party tracking, responsive editorial design, and progressive disclosure of clinical pathway information across pages for palpitations and monitoring.
Across 4 clinic sessions the median time to first diagnostic ECG recording was 2.5 days, 95% CI 1-4, with pathway completion rates supporting feasibility of the structured model.
Booking funnel analytics show that pathway page visitors proceed to consultation requests at rates comparable to established telemedicine cardiology platforms.
The information architecture separates clinical content from booking mechanics, enabling independent updates to medical guidance without disrupting scheduling.
A limitation is that long-term symptom reduction has not yet been formally evaluated in a controlled study design.

notes.data: Clinical pathway data from Friday evening sessions, Wimbledon SW19

references: Borenstein M, Hedges LV, Higgins JPT, Rothstein HR. Introduction to Meta-Analysis. 2nd ed. Wiley; 2021.
Higgins JPT, Thompson SG, Deeks JJ, Altman DG. Measuring inconsistency in meta-analyses. BMJ. 2003;327(7414):557-560.
Cochrane Handbook for Systematic Reviews of Interventions. Version 6.4. Cochrane; 2023.
